og.title: Drying Industrial & Manufacturing Sites — Free CARSI Course
og.description: A free introduction to drying and restoring flood-affected industrial and manufacturing sites: containment, VOC air-quality monitoring, drying complex materials and securing regulatory clearance.

faq.q: What does the Introduction to Drying Industrial and Manufacturing Sites course cover?
faq.a: It is a free CARSI course on drying and restoring flood-affected industrial and manufacturing sites — protecting inventory and equipment, containing contaminants, monitoring air quality for volatile organic compounds (VOCs), drying complex materials, and meeting regulatory compliance requirements.

faq.q: How are complex materials dried after flooding at a manufacturing site?
faq.a: The course covers material-specific methods: desiccant drying pouches to draw subsurface humidity from dense metals such as aluminium moulds, replacing waterlogged porous insulations to prevent microbial growth, infrared thermal mapping to dry textured plastics evenly, and negative-pressure cycling to remove moisture from composite materials.

faq.q: How is a site cleared to resume operations after water damage?
faq.a: The course explains securing clearance certification through multistage quantitative indoor air-quality testing, comparing results against pre-disruption baseline reports to confirm contaminant concentrations have been reduced and the site meets environmental compliance before permits are reinstated.

It suits Australian restoration professionals, facility managers and emergency-response teams handling water-damage recovery at industrial or manufacturing premises. It is an introductory online course with no prior experience required.
